_comment: Canonical 1-100 suitability ratings for this role — the SOURCE OF TRUTH. HTML (dossier headlines + by-role ladders + peer tables) must match. Editorial judgement, not coriolis game data. Rebuild: scripts/compute-ship-ratings.py · push to HTML: scripts/reconcile-ratings-html.py · verify: scripts/audit-ratings-consistency.py

scorecard.verdict: It tops the ladder by doing every job at capital scale in one rank-free hull, but stays top-tier rather than apex — specialists out-fight, out-haul and out-jump it, and the large pad and very-heavy cost weigh on flexibility.

factors.why: Twelve optional internals (7·6·6·6·5·5·5·4·4·4·2·1), a military slot and eight utility mounts — no other multipurpose hull carries this much room, feeding ~470 t of cargo or any role's full kit.
Eight hardpoints (1×Huge 3×Large 2×Medium 2×Small) fed by a class-8 distributor — a genuine warship battery; only a dedicated Corvette out-fights it.
~78 LY engineered as an explorer — capital reach few hulls match; only the lighter Mandalay out-jumps it, and without the cargo or guns.
No rank or permit gate and free module swaps at any station, but ~200M+ Cr to outfit and a very heavy materials bill make re-roling the costliest in the field.
Engineered shields past ~1,600 MJ over stacked hull reinforcement — a heavy tank for any job — offset by slow, ponderous handling and a large pad that locks out outposts.

scorecard.verdict: Best-armed, deepest-holding medium generalist — three large hardpoints, ~294 t and ~1,100 MJ shields on a no-rank medium pad — held off the top only by a modest ~45 LY range.

factors.why: Ten optional internals (6·6·6·5·5·4·3·3·2·1) swallow any role's full kit — ~294 t cargo, a complete mining suite, or exploration gear — the deepest internal bay of any versatile medium; only the large all-rounders carry more.

3L + 2M hardpoints plus 4 utility mounts make it a genuine warship, not a token-armed trader — the heaviest hardpoint loadout among versatile mediums, outgunning the ~318 t Corsair and the lighter-armed Phantom.

~45 LY engineered as an explorer is its clear weakness — the Krait Phantom and Mandalay jump far further, and even laden the Python sits well below the medium field's range leaders. The factor that caps the score.

Medium pad lands at most stations, no rank or permit, ~55.3M Cr hull; one engineered hull (~90M Cr) re-roles between warship, trader, miner and explorer by swapping stored module sets — capital-like versatility for far less than an Anaconda.

Strong ~294 MJ base shield rising to ~1,100+ MJ engineered, stackable HRPs and a 5A cell bank make it tough across every job; handling is the trade-off — it is slow and ponderous next to the faster Kraits.

scorecard.verdict: Three large hardpoints plus an SLF bay make it the hardest-hitting versatile medium with rank-free medium-pad access; only its modest jump range holds it below the Python and Anaconda.

factors.why: Optional internals run 6·6·5·5·4·3·3·2·1 with two size-6 slots — one carries the 6D Fighter Hangar, one the 6C bi-weave — leaving room for ~230 t cargo, mining or exploration kit. Broad for a medium, though a class behind the Python's ~294 t and the large all-rounders.
3 large + 2 medium hardpoints plus a ship-launched fighter bay is the most firepower of any versatile medium; matches the Python's three large guns and adds a deployable wingman the Python lacks.

Jump range is modest — explicitly the hull's weak axis, far short of the Krait Phantom and Mandalay. An Increased-Range 5A FSD keeps it usable across trade/explore swaps but it never approaches the Anaconda's ~78 LY.

Medium pad with no rank or permit and ~45.2M Cr hull (cheaper than the Python); one engineered hull re-roles to combat, trade, mining or exploration by swapping stored module sets, ~78M Cr fully kitted.

Fast for a medium at 245 m/s / 337 boost (~400 engineered) behind a 6C bi-weave with two shield boosters, HRP and SCB; speed dictates or escapes engagements, but cargo and raw shield strength trail the Python.

scorecard.verdict: Premium ~75 LY range plus deep internals and a cheap, rank-free medium hull lead; self-defence-only firepower (2L·2M, no fighter bay) is the limiter.

factors.why: Deep optional internals (6·5·5·5·3·3·3·2·1) carry a full expedition kit plus cargo or a mining suite, but cap at size 6 and ~190t — the least cargo of the top versatile mediums (Python ~294t, Corsair ~318t).
Two large and two medium hardpoints (2L·2M) with four utilities, but no third large mount and no fighter bay — combat is self-defence, not dominance; the Mk II and Python out-gun it.
~75 LY engineered is the signature stat — far beyond any other versatile medium and bettered only by the dedicated Mandalay; a premier explorer that anchors its all-round case.

Cheapest of the top versatile mediums at ~35.7M Cr hull, no rank or permit gate, medium pad lands almost anywhere, and storable role module sets let one hull re-role between explorer, trader and miner.

6C bi-weave plus two Heavy-Duty boosters and HRPs give solid survivability across roles, and 256/358 m/s (~400 boost engineered) outruns trouble it can't out-gun — but it is a medium hull, not a combat tank.

scorecard.verdict: Six hardpoints, ten optionals and a ~318 t hold lead the versatile mediums, but a weak ~42 LY jump range and only four utilities cap it below the Python.

factors.why: Ten optional internals (6·6·6·5·5·5·4·3·2·1) feed a ~318 t hold that out-carries the Python (~294 t) on a medium pad, leaving room for shield, cells and hull reinforcements simultaneously. Among versatile mediums only large-pad hulls hold more.
Three large and three medium hardpoints (six mounts) exceed the Python and Krait Mk II gun count, the most firepower of any versatile medium. The 7A plant and 7A distributor sustain all six together.

Only ~28 LY laden initial, ~36 LY A-rated and ~42 LY engineered on a 265 t frame with a 5-size FSD — a clear weakness that rules out exploration. The Phantom and Mandalay range far further in the same class.
No rank or permit gate and deep internals let one hull swap between combat, trade and mining at a station. Offset by being the priciest versatile medium at ~76.9M Cr hull, ~115M Cr engineered.

Light 265 t hull boosts 355 m/s (~420 m/s engineered) for strong handling, but the size-6 bi-weave trails the Python's apex shielding and only four utility mounts limit booster/chaff/heatsink stacking.

scorecard.verdict: Apex shields, ~794 t cargo, a huge-mount battery and 323 m/s boost make it a class-leading capital all-rounder; a ~28 LY jump range and Duke gate are the only real drags.

factors.why: Optional internals run 8·8·6·6·6·5·5·4·3·1 plus two class-5 military slots and 8 utility mounts, yielding ~794 t cargo or any role module set — the deepest internal layout in the field.
Seven hardpoints (1 huge, 2 large, 4 medium) anchored by the single huge mount give genuine conflict-zone firepower, second only to the Corvette among large all-rounders.
Even engineered with Increased Range, max jump is only ~28 LY (~16 stock), the worst trait of the hull and the one role — exploration — it cannot serve.
One A-rated hull re-roles between combat, trade and mining via storable module sets, but ~200M Cr hull (~280M+ engineered), the Imperial Duke gate and large-pad lock raise the entry and refit cost.
Base ~703 MJ shield (highest in game) reaches ~2,500+ MJ engineered with a prismatic and five boosters; 323 m/s boost and Dirty Drives keep a capital hull unexpectedly agile.

scorecard.verdict: The finest small-pad all-rounder — five hardpoints, nine optionals and small-pad access cover every role, limited only by the cargo and combat ceiling of any medium multirole.

factors.why: Nine optional internals (5·4·4·4·3·3·3·2·1) and ~110 t max cargo make it the roomiest small-pad hull, swapping freely between cargo, cabin, mining or exploration; only mediums (Python ~284 t, Anaconda ~500 t) carry meaningfully more.
Five hardpoints (3 Medium · 2 Small) is class-leading firepower for a small ship — enough to clear low/medium RES — but three mediums cap below a medium multirole's large mounts.
~38 LY engineered laden jump (FSD4 Increased Range) is solid for a 150 t hull and beats most small multiroles, but trails dedicated explorers like the DBX and Asp Explorer.

~1.48M Cr hull, no rank or permit gate, small pad docking anywhere, plus nine optionals to re-role — engineer the cores once and swap modules per task; among the cheapest, most flexible re-fits in the field.

Four utilities allow a full defensive suite and ~300 MJ engineered shield on a bi-weave, with 415 m/s (engineered ~480) boost for evasion; thin small-hull armour keeps it below dedicated combat hulls.

scorecard.verdict: It earns 78 on capital-scale internals, the best firepower in the role and an un-killable tank, held back chiefly by a class-6 FSD that caps jumps near 31 LY plus rank and pad gates.

factors.why: Eleven optional internals (7·7·7·6·6·5·5·4·4·3·1) plus two class-5 military slots — capital-scale volume that holds ~618 t of cargo, a full mining suite or a fortress fit, more optional slots than the Anaconda or Cutter.
Eight hardpoint mounts (2H·1L·2M·2S) anchored by two huge slots — the heaviest gun array of any multipurpose hull, and the two huge mounts stay fitted in every configuration.
A class-6 FSD dragging a 900-tonne hull caps jumps near ~31 LY even fully engineered (~17 stock) — a clear weakness against the Anaconda and a poor explorer.

Module-swap re-tuning works at a station, but the large pad bars outposts, Federal Rear Admiral gates ownership, and ~183M Cr hull rising past 260M+ engineered makes re-fitting across roles expensive.

~555 MJ base shield reaching ~2,000+ MJ engineered over a 900t hull with two military slots and deep HRPs — the best tank in any role; handling stays manageable A-rated but the mass remains ponderous.

scorecard.verdict: A 78 is carried by class-leading 85+ LY range, ten optionals and four mediums on a fast, gate-free hull, capped by a soft ~220 MJ frame and lighter ~154 t capacity.

factors.why: Ten optional internals (6·5·4·4·3·3·2·1·1·1) plus four utilities give real do-everything slot count, but ~154 t max cargo trails the Python (~294 t), Krait Mk II (~230 t) and Phantom (~190 t); broad but smaller-volume than the heavyweight mediums.

Four medium plus two small hardpoints, all gimballable — more medium guns than most generalists, but no large mounts and a PP5/PD5 core that limits the brawl. Genuine RES teeth, below the combat-first Python and Krait.

~85+ LY engineered laden jump on a 230 t frame with the stock SCO drive — class-leading range and 280/350 m/s travel speed beat every rival medium multirole; nothing in the field reaches or crosses systems faster.

No rank or permit gate and a ~16.5M Cr hull; re-roles by swapping optionals (cargo, cabin, mining, expedition) with core engineering done once. Low mass means even a loaded fit keeps usable range — cheap, fast re-fit.

~220 MJ base shield (~430 engineered) and a light 230 t hull make it a softer brawler than a Python or Krait; survivability rests on 280/350 m/s speed and nimble handling rather than tank — forgiving to fly, fragile if it stands and trades.

scorecard.verdict: A fast, cheap large hull whose nine optional slots and Imperial-Baron access carry it, but only four hardpoints, no military slot and a class-5 FSD cap it.

factors.why: Nine optional internals (7·6·4·4·3·3·2·2·1) plus four utility mounts give genuine room for the price, but ~250 t cargo and the absent military slot trail the Anaconda (~470 t) and Cutter (~794 t) and limit a defensive reinforcement stack.
Only two Large and two Medium hardpoints, no military slot — half the Anaconda's eight mounts. Fine for light combat, but clearly below the genuine generalists at sustained firepower.
A class-5 FSD reaches only ~24 LY A-rated and ~34 LY engineered — usable for travel and errands but well short of an Asp (~58 LY) or DBX, a recurring multipurpose limiter.
At ~21M Cr hull (under many mediums) and gated only by Imperial Baron, it re-tunes cheaply between hauling, light combat and exploration; the large-pad-only footprint locks it out of outpost work.
The fastest large ship — 380 m/s boost stock, ~460 engineered — with medium-ship agility for escaping fights it can't win; a ~620 MJ engineered bi-weave is solid, though no military slot caps armour.

scorecard.verdict: A ~208k Cr small-pad hull with eight optionals, ~50 LY range and 403/480 m/s pace earns broad versatility cheaply; light 2M·2S firepower is the ceiling.

factors.why: Eight optional internals (4·4·4·2·2·2·1·1) cover guns, ~64 t cargo, a size-4 bi-weave, scoop or SRV, but the largest slot is only size 4 — versatile yet modest, mid-field for the role.
Only 2 medium + 2 small hardpoints and 2 utility mounts cap it at light combat; combat capability stays 'light' even fully engineered, the clear weakest dimension versus the medium all-rounders.
~32 LY stock rising to ~50 LY engineered as an explorer fit is strong for a small all-rounder — beats the Courier and Adder, trailing only the dedicated DBX in class.
~208k Cr hull, ~5M Cr all-in engineered, no rank or permit, and a small pad that lands anywhere — re-roles between combat/trade/explore by swapping optionals; role-leading on cost and access.

Light size-4 bi-weave shield, single shield booster across just 2 utilities and a 2D hull reinforcement make it fragile, but 403 m/s boost (~480 engineered) and nimble handling let it disengage at will.

scorecard.verdict: Ten optional internals, a ~92t hold and five hardpoints make it the most capacious cheap small all-rounder, but lost speed, light shields and only two utilities cap it.

factors.why: Ten optional internals (4·4·4·4·3·3·2·2·1·1) and a ~92t hold out-carry the Mk III by half again — strong breadth for a small hull, though absolute slot sizes stay modest beside any medium.
Five hardpoints (2M·3S) — one more than the Mk III, enough kinetic-plus-laser pressure for light bounties and mission combat, but small-class mounts cap real damage output.
~50 LY engineered as an explorer (Increased Range FSD on a size-4 drive), ~42 LY A-rated — solid travel and light-exploration legs for a cheap all-rounder, short of the dedicated DBX/Asp range.
~623k Cr hull, ~6M all-in, no rank or permit gate, and a small pad that lands anywhere — re-role between cargo, guns or a fuel scoop across ten optionals at the lowest cost of any Cobra.

The clear limiter: 200/300 m/s on a heavier frame than the Mk III, light bi-weave shield and only two utility mounts — slow, soft and modest by medium standards despite a 4C bi-weave and hull reinforcement.

scorecard.verdict: Class-leading ~62 LY range, small-pad reach and sub-15M-Cr value carry it; light 1L/2M firepower and thin small-ship internals cap the heavy end and hold it to 68.

factors.why: Eight optional internals (4·4·3·3·2·2·1·1) with no military slots and only ~60t max cargo — enough to mix a shield, a couple of cargo racks, scoop, SRV and scanner, but capacity sits well below the do-everything mediums and larges like the Asp Explorer (~128t) or Python (~284t).

One large plus two medium hardpoints and four utility mounts — real teeth for self-defence and low-intensity bounty work, but light: it can't out-fight a Cobra Mk V or the multirole mediums, and the single large mount caps burst.
~62 LY multirole-loaded — explorer-grade reach that no other small-pad generalist near its price matches, and it out-ranges the do-everything mediums; the FSD is the hull's signature edge.

~1.64M Cr hull and ~15M Cr fully engineered, no rank or permit, and a small pad that docks at any outpost; the few optionals re-tune cheaply between roaming, light combat and hauling — only the thin slot count limits how much it swaps at once.
Agile, cool-running and quick to turn at a station, but thin armour, no military slot and a modest ~350 MJ engineered bi-weave shield mean it defends and dabbles rather than tanks.

scorecard.verdict: Seven optional internals and a near-free, rank-free ~41k hull buy genuine do-anything breadth, but ~30t cargo, 1M+2S of weak guns and a 321 m/s boost cap its ceiling.

factors.why: Seven optional internals (3·3·2·2·1·1·1) plus 2 utilities let one hull carry a small hold, shield, scoop, scanner or SRV and re-role to any activity — broad coverage, but every slot is size 3 or smaller so each role is fitted only modestly.
Just 1 medium + 2 small hardpoints and 2 utilities; combat rates weak across all three states. Adequate to swat a single low threat, but among small all-rounders the Cobras carry far more gun.
Light hull reaches ~47 LY fully fitted (~28 stock, ~38 A-rated) — a strong figure for so cheap a ship and its best objective stat, behind only dedicated explorers like the DBX in this field.

~41k Cr hull, no rank or permit, trivial rebuy, full multirole ~3.5M all-in, and a small pad that lands anywhere; tiny optionals swap in minutes to sample any role at zero risk — its defining strength.

Only a 3C bi-weave plus a single 0A booster for shields, light build, and a slow 221/321 m/s top/boost; lightly armed and lightly armoured, it survives only weak threats and handles sluggishly until thrusters are tuned.

scorecard.verdict: A sub-3M-credit, rank-free medium that hauls, mines, scouts and fights a little with a unique cheap fighter bay; light firepower and class-5-capped internals keep it mid-field.

factors.why: Optional internals top out at class 5 (5·5·4·3·2·2·1) with 3 utility mounts, capping cargo at ~98 t and module room, but it uniquely fits a size-5 SLF hangar plus shield, HRP and scanner. Real variety on a budget, yet small inside versus the proper mediums.

Only 2M + 2S hardpoints for light gimballed guns; engineered combat is modest even with Overcharged multi-cannons. The SLF adds a second gun platform, but raw firepower trails every endgame all-rounder.

~30 LY engineered (~16 stock) on a maxed Increased-Range FSD is serviceable for trade legs and scouting, sitting mid-field — well short of an Asp Explorer or DBX but ahead of the small budget hulls' light fits.

~2.95M Cr hull, no rank or permit, A-rated multirole ~7M Cr, full kit ~15M Cr. Cheap stored module sets swap it between haul, mine, scout and fight at a station — re-fit cost is its standout strength.

Combat-hardened airframe and a 4C bi-weave reaching ~300+ MJ engineered make it far tougher than a Type-6, but it is slow (200 m/s, ~340 boost engineered) and turns lazily, so handling drags survivability down.
